prompt: Files.readString throws NoSuchFileException. What is the bug?
choices: Files.readString(Path) throws NoSuchFileException when the file does not exist at the given path; there is no null-return fallback
Files.readString is only available in Java 11+; use Files.readAllBytes on older versions
Relative paths are resolved from the classpath root, not the working directory
explanation: Files.readString() throws NoSuchFileException (a subtype of IOException) if the file doesn't exist — it does not return null or an empty string. Fix: check Files.exists(p) before reading, or catch IOException and handle the missing-file case.
